items.title: Iran war unlikely to trigger terror attacks in Indonesia: ex-militants
NATO seeks clarity as US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid rift with Europe
items.description: Trump says he is considering pulling US troops from Italy and Spain as well, due to their opposition to the war against Iran; NATO says it shows Europe's need to invest in defense The post NATO seeks clarity as US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid rift with Europe appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Israeli air strikes kill 10 people in southern Lebanon
items.description: Drones and planes hit several towns despite 'ceasefire' since April 17 as Hezbollah pledges to continue its attacks.

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.

items.title: Beirut, è salito a 13 il bilancio delle vittime dei raid israeliani
items.description: Nonostante il cessate il fuoco tra Hezbollah e Israele, in vigore dal 17 aprile

items.title: U.S. to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany in next 6-12 months
items.description: The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Donald Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran official says renewed conflict ‘likely’, Trump says fighting ‘terminated’
items.description: Senior Iranian military official Mohammad Jafar Asadi on Saturday suggested a renewed conflict with the US was “likely” as peace talks between Tehran and Washington continued to stall. US President Donald Trump on Friday told top US lawmakers that hostilities between Iran and the US had “terminated” since the ceasefire…

items.title: Cuba calls US sanctions 'collective punishment' during May 1 marches
items.description: In an executive order on May 1, US President Donald Trump announced sanctions on people involved in wide-sweeping sectors of the Cuban economy, including those who operate in 'energy and 'defense.' Cuba's foreign minister called the new measures 'collective punishment' of the nation's people.

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.description: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: Is Myanmar's military winning the civil war?
items.description: After five years of war, armed resistance groups still challenge Myanmar's military across much of the country. But the generals are now stemming losses and reclaiming some territory.

items.title: Is our home safe? Is it rubble? For displaced Lebanese, anguished uncertainty.
items.description: Lebanese people displaced by war have been on an emotional roller coaster. A ceasefire has been extended, but fighting in the south hasn’t ended. Even as families yearning for home prepare to return, despite the uncertainty, they are warned not to take the risk.

items.title: Oil price tops $126 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
items.description: Markets spooked as US president appears willing to keep up naval blockade and Iran keeps strait of Hormuz all but shutThe global oil price hit $126 a barrel on Thursday, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump said the US blockade of Iranian ports could last for months and peace talks remained stalled.After su…

items.title: Lebanon strikes deepen crisis as hunger rises and conditions worsen in Gaza
items.description: At least nine people were reported to have been killed in southern Lebanon on Thursday, as ongoing hostilities continue to exact a heavy toll on civilians and drive a worsening humanitarian crisis marked by rising hunger and strained public services.

items.title: Ukraine Strikes Deep Inside Russia, Tuapse Hit Again
items.description: Ukraine has hit an oil facility and industrial site in Russia's Perm region, roughly 1,500 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, according to Russian officials, as US President Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin discussed a potential temporary cease-fire aimed at pausing the four-year war in Ukraine.
